title: Thevetia nitida
intro: Thevetia nitida is a small, deciduous tree native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The health benefits of Thevetia nitida include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include thevetin, thevetin A, and thevetin B, as well as other alkaloids and glycosides. The most common herbal preparations made with Thevetia nitida include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Thevetia nitida is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Thevetia nitida is classified as a deciduous shrub or small tree with compound leaves. The plant is also known for its showy, white or yellow flowers, which are highly fragrant and attract pollinators. Its classification places it within the Apocynaceae family, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbanes or oleanders.
uses: Thevetia nitida is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Traditionally, extracts from the plant have been used in some cultures to treat various ailments, including fever and inflammation. The plant is said to contain compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Some studies suggest that thevetia nitida extracts may have antimicrobial and antiproliferative effects, although these findings require further validation. Further research is necessary to fully understand the medicinal uses of thevetia nitida and to establish its safety for human use.

preparations: Thevetia nitida is sometimes used in herbal preparations, although it's essential to note that this plant contains toxic compounds. However, the use of Thevetia nitida in herbal preparations should be approached with caution due to its toxicity. Its toxic compounds, including thevetin and thevetin-A, can be hazardous to human health if ingested in large quantities. side_effects: Thevetia nitida is known to be toxic due to the presence of thevetin and other compounds in its leaves and seeds. Ingestion of any part of the plant can cause severe symptoms, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. If ingested in large quantities, Thevetia nitida can lead to more serious health issues, such as cardiac problems and even death. The plant's sap can also cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. It is essential to exercise extreme caution and avoid any contact with Thevetia nitida.
